description_long: This clock tower features a tall, tiered black structure with a prominent, angular spire at the top, rendered in a metallic gray. The base is rectangular and solid, with a step-like transition to a narrower upper section, giving the tower a monumental and imposing appearance. The spire is supported by four open, vertical struts that create a sense of lightness above the otherwise massive base. There are no visible windows, doors, or decorative elements on the main body, emphasizing a minimalist and modern aesthetic. The clock face is not visible from any angle, suggesting it may be absent or located on a side not shown. The overall design is geometric and streamlined, with sharp edges and a strong vertical emphasis.
description: A tall, black, tiered clock tower with a metallic gray spire supported by open struts, featuring a minimalist and modern design. No visible clock face or decorative details.
description_view_0: Front view shows the stepped black base and the open, angular gray spire structure above.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the same stepped base and spire, with the open struts of the spire more clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the stepped base and spire, but no additional features are visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the symmetry and open spire structure.
